title: cincinalis fendleri
intro: Cincinalis fendleri, also known as Fendler's prickly poppy or Fendler's prickly poppy mallow, is a perennial plant that belongs to the mallow family and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant contains alkaloids with medicinal properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Cincinalis fendleri include its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help soothe digestive issues and relieve pain. Herbal preparations of Cincinalis fendleri can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can be used externally as a poultice or salve to treat skin irritations and wounds.
